source_snippet:
This is the FAaaS workstream to address critical security vulnerabilities from Mythos scan, resolve issues and assist in fleet rollout activities.

* FAaaS PMO ticket: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/browse/FAAASPMO-1263>
* Internal FAaaS slack chanel: [#internal-faaas-scan-kiwi](https://oracle.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C08PB5J87C5)
* External FAaaS slack channel with SaaS Security POCs: [#apps-division-scan-kiwi](https://oracle.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0AUEDVPU8K)

  


FAaaS Kiwi Workstreams
----------------------

### #1 Security scanning and fixing critical findings

1. Current scan status: 89% complete (237 of 266 scanned, 29 remaining) -  [SaaS dashboard link](https://saasinsights.oraclecorp.com/ords/r/saaskpi/a1004/kiwi-saas-security-dashboard)
2. FAaaS Repos to scan: 266 FAaaS repos to provided SaaS security in new format: 
	1. Sampath has provided 28 additional FAaaS RM repos for FAAASSRE JIRA - [devops-coderepository link](https://devops.oci.oraclecorp.com/devops-coderepository/projects/ocid1.devopsproject.oc1.phx.amaaaaaaw4vcxbyalo5gavleayvmmkytburb5xxytnb5ysd3ltvnwdryqztq/repositories?_ctx=us-phoenix-1%2Cdevops_scm_central)
3. FAaaS Jiras:
	1. FAaaS Kiwi JIRA dashboard: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/secure/Dashboard.jspa?selectPageId=73429>
	2. FAaaS Kiwi JIRA filter: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/issues/?filter=219921>
4. Working group with engineers to accelerate the issue triage and resolution process. Sunil has scheduled on Monday 5/18
	1. ADP: Sang, Norman
	2. FACP: Matthias
	3. HCP: Arup, Abhay, Fang

### #2 Fleet Patching

1. Leadership ask is to come up with a proposal for monthly full fleet patching in June and beyond. Options are under review (**Siju**, **Venkatesh**) - [Patching proposal image](https://confluence.oraclecorp.com/confluence/download/attachments/20352556426/image-2026-5-7_18-0-13.png?version=1&modificationDate=1778191217000&api=v2)
2. Patching proposal reviewed on 5/12 Strategic Ops -
3. From Preeti, for the June tactical + future support for fleetwide patching, there are some ERs we need filed / tracked as part of the program as far as Refresh goes:
	1. We need to support bulk cancel API to get pre-existing conflicting schedules out of the way.
	2. We need to get a finalized blurb for customer notification assuming we want to notify the customer why their Refresh was canceled.

### #3 DB Patching

1. One round of Ksplice patching done:
	1. PROD Clusters: Total: 854/855 Completed, Pending: 1 (eiaa1a030 - Inactive cluster . Nothing to patch)
	2. Non-PROD Clusters (Pre-Prod, PINT, Beta): All Completed (209/209)
	3. DEMO Clusters: All Completed (93/93)
2. For non-Ksplice patching, Currently only 2 slots of 3 days each are available per month. In order to do full fleet, Sravan needs 5 slots of 3 days each
3. We need clarity from leadership if DB patching every month is a requirement and exactly what types of patching (DomU, Dom0, GI)

### #4 OS Patching

1. Ed is working with Erna's team on OS patching of Linux image

Appendix - Supporting Materials
-------------------------------

FAaaS repo list: <https://confluence.oraclecorp.com/confluence/display/OCIFACP/FAaaS+Repos+for+Security+Scanning>

SaaS Kiwi dashboard: [https://saasinsights.oraclecorp.com/ords/r/saaskpi/a1004/kiwi-saas-security-dashboard](https://oracle-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/mauruthi_geetha_mohan_oracle_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BC3BB40F4-2031-48A1-B6E0-353419DCCEB7%7D&file=kiwi-priority.xlsx&wdLOR=c1E39D1D1-9F92-0444-A12F-1DA0E11A44AA&nav=MTVfe0IwQzdFNERGLUYzNjUtNEM3Ri1BQTA3LTVCMDREMTgxNkI4Q30&action=default&mobileredirect=true)

SaaS Kiwi xls: [kiwi-priority.xlsx](https://oracle-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/mauruthi_geetha_mohan_oracle_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BC3BB40F4-2031-48A1-B6E0-353419DCCEB7%7D&file=kiwi-priority.xlsx&wdLOR=c1E39D1D1-9F92-0444-A12F-1DA0E11A44AA&nav=MTVfe0IwQzdFNERGLUYzNjUtNEM3Ri1BQTA3LTVCMDREMTgxNkI4Q30&action=default&mobileredirect=true)

expected_extraction.reporting_date: As of 5/18 (inferred from latest dated update)
expected_extraction.project_purpose: Address critical security vulnerabilities identified by Mythos scan by completing security scanning, fixing critical findings, and supporting fleet rollout patching activities for FAaaS (Functions as a Service).
expected_extraction.rag_summary: Amber — Security scanning is 89% complete (237/266; 29 remaining) and DB Ksplice is essentially complete, but fleet/DB patching decisions and capacity gaps (only 2 monthly non‑Ksplice slots available vs 5 needed) plus pending leadership clarity on monthly patching requirements create schedule risk.
expected_extraction.schedule_health: The program has near-term momentum with 89% of repos scanned (29 remaining) and an engineer working group scheduled for 5/18 to accelerate triage. Schedule risk remains because fleet patching options are still under review and non‑Ksplice DB patching capacity (2 slots/month vs 5 needed) can delay full-fleet completion.
status_highlights: Security scanning 89% complete; 237/266 repos scanned (29 remaining) with SaaS Kiwi dashboard tracking.
Working group to accelerate triage/resolution scheduled for Monday 5/18 (ADP, FACP, HCP participants listed).
DB Ksplice patching completed for Non-PROD (209/209) and DEMO (93/93); PROD 854/855 complete with 1 inactive/pending cluster.
Fleet patching: proposal for monthly full fleet patching (June and beyond) under review; reviewed on 5/12 Strategic Ops.
Non‑Ksplice DB patching constrained to 2 slots/month (3 days each) vs 5 slots/month needed for full fleet.

workstreams.name: #1 Security scanning and fixing critical findings
workstreams.status: 89% complete (237/266 repos scanned; 29 remaining) with tracking via SaaS Kiwi security dashboard; repo list maintained and additional repos provided.
workstreams.dependency_or_decision: Engineer working group scheduled for 5/18 to accelerate issue triage and resolution across ADP/FACP/HCP.

workstreams.name: #2 Fleet Patching
workstreams.status: Monthly full fleet patching proposal for June and beyond is in progress; options under review and proposal was reviewed on 5/12 (Strategic Ops).
workstreams.dependency_or_decision: Leadership decision needed on monthly full fleet patching approach; ERs required for bulk cancel API and finalized customer notification blurb.

workstreams.name: #3 DB Patching
workstreams.status: One round of Ksplice patching done: PROD 854/855 complete (1 inactive/pending), Non-PROD 209/209 complete, DEMO 93/93 complete.
workstreams.dependency_or_decision: Capacity gap for non‑Ksplice patching (2 slots/month available vs 5 needed) and leadership clarity needed on whether monthly DB patching is required and which patch types (DomU, Dom0, GI).

workstreams.name: #4 OS Patching
workstreams.status: OS patching of Linux image in progress (Ed working with Erna’s team).
workstreams.dependency_or_decision: Depends on coordination with Erna’s team to complete Linux image OS patching.

blocking_issues.description: Decide and approve proposal for monthly full fleet patching in June and beyond (options under review).
File/track ERs needed for fleetwide patching support: bulk cancel API to remove conflicting schedules; finalize customer notification blurb for canceled Refresh.
Resolve non‑Ksplice DB patching capacity gap: only 2 slots (3 days each) available per month vs 5 slots needed for full fleet.
Get leadership clarity on whether monthly DB patching is required and which patching types apply (DomU, Dom0, GI).

next_steps.action: Use the 5/18 working group to accelerate triage and drive closure plans for critical findings.
Complete remaining security scans (29 repos) and ensure all 266 repos are provided to SaaS Security in the required format.
Finalize and socialize the monthly full fleet patching proposal for June and beyond based on options under review.
File and track ERs for bulk cancel API and finalize the customer notification blurb for canceled Refresh.
Confirm whether monthly DB patching is required and define patching types (DomU, Dom0, GI) to plan non‑Ksplice execution.
Progress OS patching of the Linux image and align on remaining work to complete.

exec_asks: Approve a proposal for monthly full fleet patching starting in June so teams can lock cadence and execution plans.
Provide clarity on whether DB patching every month is required and which patching types (DomU, Dom0, GI) apply so Sravan can plan non‑Ksplice capacity and scheduling.
key_metrics: DB Ksplice PROD clusters completed/pending: 854/855 (pending 1: eiaa1a030 inactive)
Non-Ksplice DB patching capacity available: 2 slots/month (3 days each)
Non-Ksplice DB patching slots needed for full fleet: 5 slots/month (3 days each)
